docx:
Hylonomus is one of the earliest known reptiles.
It lived during the Carboniferous period.
Shows early adaptation to terrestrial life.

Hylonomus lived about 315 million years ago.
Fossils were found in North America.
It represents early amniote evolution.

Level of Organisation: Organ system level
Germ layer: Triploblastic
Body symmetry: Bilateral
Coelom: True coelom
Body plan: Tube within a tube body plan (Deuterostomous)
Digestion type: Complete
Respiratory type: Pulmonary respiration
Circulatory type: Closed, double, incomplete double
Circulation system: Closed
Regulation of Osmosis: Osmoregulator
Excretory organ: Kidneys
Mode of excretion: Uricotelism
Skeleton structure: Endoskeleton
Nervous system: Moderately developed
Metamerism: Absent
Fertilization: Internal
Development type: Direct (oviparous)
Body temperature: Poikilothermic

Kingdom: Animalia
Phylum: Chordata
Class: Reptilia
Order: Captorhinida
Family: Captorhinidae
Genus: Hylonomus
Species: —

• Small lizard-like body
• Slender limbs
• Long tail
• Sharp teeth

• Insect predator
• Early terrestrial vertebrate
• Forest floor inhabitant

• Important fossil in reptile evolution
• Helps trace origin of amniotes
• Paleontological significance

Climatius was an early jawed fish.
Belonged to Acanthodii group.
Had multiple fin spines.

Lived during the Devonian period.
Fossils found in Europe.
Represents early jawed vertebrates.

Level of Organisation: Organ system level
Germ layer: Triploblastic
Body symmetry: Bilateral
Coelom: True coelom
Body plan: Tube within a tube body plan (Deuterostomous)
Digestion type: Complete
Respiratory type: Branchial respiration
Circulatory type: Closed, single
Circulation system: Closed
Regulation of Osmosis: Osmoregulator
Excretory organ: Kidneys
Mode of excretion: Ammonotelism
Skeleton structure: Cartilaginous endoskeleton
Nervous system: Well developed
Metamerism: Present
Fertilization: External
Development type: Indirect
Body temperature: Poikilothermic

Kingdom: Animalia
Phylum: Chordata
Class: Acanthodii
Order: Climatiiformes
Family: Climatiidae
Genus: Climatius
Species: —

• Streamlined body
• Multiple fin spines
• Small size
• Shark-like appearance

• Small aquatic predator
• Early jawed fish radiation
• Freshwater ecosystem member

• Fossil evidence of fish evolution
• Study of jaw development
• Paleontological importance

Cephalaspis was a jawless armored fish.
Belonged to Ostracodermi.
Had bony head shield.

Lived during the Devonian period.
Fossils found in Europe and North America.
Represents early vertebrate evolution.

Level of Organisation: Organ system level
Germ layer: Triploblastic
Body symmetry: Bilateral
Coelom: True coelom
Body plan: Tube within a tube body plan (Deuterostomous)
Digestion type: Complete
Respiratory type: Branchial respiration
Circulatory type: Closed, single
Circulation system: Closed
Regulation of Osmosis: Osmoregulator
Excretory organ: Kidneys
Mode of excretion: Ammonotelism
Skeleton structure: Bony exoskeletal plates
Nervous system: Primitive vertebrate type
Metamerism: Present
Fertilization: External
Development type: Indirect
Body temperature: Poikilothermic

Kingdom: Animalia
Phylum: Chordata
Class: Ostracodermi
Order: Cephalaspidiformes
Family: Cephalaspididae
Genus: Cephalaspis
Species: —

• Broad armored head
• Jawless mouth
• Flattened body
• Tail fin present

• Bottom feeder
• Early vertebrate lineage
• Freshwater inhabitant

• Important fossil fish
• Evidence for vertebrate evolution
• Paleo-studies

Albatross is a large seabird.
Has very long wings.
Excellent glider.

Found in southern oceans.
Feeds on fish and squid.
Known for long-distance flight.

Level of Organisation: Organ system level
Germ layer: Triploblastic
Body symmetry: Bilateral
Coelom: True coelom
Body plan: Tube within a tube body plan (Deuterostomous)
Digestion type: Complete
Respiratory type: Pulmonary respiration with air sacs
Circulatory type: Closed, double, complete
Circulation system: Closed
Regulation of Osmosis: Osmoregulator
Excretory organ: Kidneys
Mode of excretion: Uricotelism
Skeleton structure: Endoskeleton
Nervous system: Well developed
Metamerism: Absent
Fertilization: Internal
Development type: Direct (oviparous)
Body temperature: Homeothermic

Kingdom: Animalia
Phylum: Chordata
Class: Aves
Order: Procellariiformes
Family: Diomedeidae
Genus: Diomedea
Species: —

• Very large wingspan
• White and black plumage
• Hooked beak
• Webbed feet

• Marine predator
• Ocean ecosystem balance
• Long-distance migratory bird

Raphus cucullatus was a flightless bird.
Endemic to Mauritius.
Extinct due to human activity.

Lived until the 17th century.
Inhabited island forests.
Example of human-caused extinction.

Level of Organisation: Organ system level
Germ layer: Triploblastic
Body symmetry: Bilateral
Coelom: True coelom
Body plan: Tube within a tube body plan (Deuterostomous)
Digestion type: Complete
Respiratory type: Pulmonary respiration with air sacs
Circulatory type: Closed, double, complete
Circulation system: Closed
Regulation of Osmosis: Osmoregulator
Excretory organ: Kidneys
Mode of excretion: Uricotelism
Skeleton structure: Endoskeleton
Nervous system: Well developed
Metamerism: Absent
Fertilization: Internal
Development type: Direct (oviparous)
Body temperature: Homeothermic

Kingdom: Animalia
Phylum: Chordata
Class: Aves
Order: Columbiformes
Family: Columbidae
Genus: Raphus
Species: Raphus cucullatus

• Large round body
• Small wings
• Strong legs
• Curved beak

• Island seed disperser
• Ground-dwelling bird
• Example of island evolution

• Symbol of extinction
• Conservation awareness
• Evolutionary research
